reasoning: Attacked by decoding the SHORT/LONG/PORT macros myself and it held. WSBASE:182-198 emit a 3-byte wedge record: byte0 = TYP$ (LONG/SHORT/PORT), byte1 = left-wall descriptor, byte2 = right-wall descriptor. Each descriptor is `.A1*4+.A2*4+.A3*4+.A4`. The finding hedged that this 'does NOT obviously produce a 4x2-bit byte' and left the packing un-decoded — I decoded it. MACRO-11 has NO operator precedence: expressions evaluate strictly left-to-right, so `.A1*4+.A2*4+.A3*4+.A4` = ((((A1*4)+A2)*4+A3)*4+A4) = A1*64 + A2*16 + A3*4 + A4 — a clean 4x2-bit pack (A1=top slot bits[7:6], A4=bottom slot bits[1:0]). The WSPANL extraction loop proves 4 stacked slots: `ANDA #3` takes the low 2-bit slot, `CMPA PN.DES` matches type, then `LSRA/LSRA` + `EQEND` walk up the byte while `M.Z0 += 400` climbs the Y=+/-400 wall (PNVLW/PNVRW :161-244). Panel-type semantics confirmed by VWPANEL (:81-121): PN.DES 1->TD$WPN decorative panel, 2->TD$WFF force field, 3->TD$WGA gun; 0=blank (never matched). Slot ordering nailed by the wedge comments themselves: TWDG92 'CATWALK AT TOP' = `SHORT 2,3,3,3` (A1=2=force-field on top), TWDG94 'CATWALK AT BOTTOM' = `SHORT 1,1,1,2` (A4=2=force-field on bottom). Our 8-entry TRENCH_OBSTACLE_STATIONS list of {turret|square|catwalk} plus uniform trenchWallDetail rectangles genuinely cannot express a per-wall 4-slot 2-bit column at any constant setting. Structural gap is real.
corrections: (1) The finding says '49 named wedge groups (TWDG01..TWDG99)'; the actual count is 53 (TWDG01-37, 41-45, 53-55, 92-99). (2) The finding's key hedge — that the packing macro 'does NOT obviously produce a 4x2-bit byte' and was 'left un-decoded rather than guessed' — is now RESOLVED: under MACRO-11 left-to-right evaluation it decodes cleanly to A1*64+A2*16+A3*4+A4, a proper 4x2-bit byte with A1=top, A4=bottom (verified by the CATWALK-AT-TOP/BOTTOM comments). The 4-slot semantics are no longer merely 'certain from the WSPANL loop' — the literal encoding is now fully decoded. (3) '11 pies (TPIE) + one random pie' is exact: TPIE = PIE1..PIE11 (WSBASE:176-178), PIEXX = RANDOM (:162). Cluster note: B-012 is a specific instance of THIS model gap (the catwalk is force-field panel type 2 inside this same grid), and one rewrite — porting the 4x2-bit panel grid — subsumes both; they are distinct observable claims (encoding vs collision behaviour), not the same defect filed twice.

reasoning: Verified the wave-selection arithmetic in NWBASE (WSBASE:1027-1036): `LDB BS.WAV / LSLB / LDX #TPIE / ABX / CMPX #TPIEZ / IFHS -> LDU #RPIE (random pie) / ELSE -> LDU (X) (predefined pie)`. LSLB doubles BS.WAV to a word index into TPIE. TPIE holds exactly 11 words PIE1..PIE11 (:176-178), so TPIEZ = TPIE+22. For BS.WAV 0..10 the index 0..20 is < 22 -> IFHS false -> a FIXED predefined pie (run-identical every run). For BS.WAV >= 11 the index >= 22 = TPIEZ -> IFHS true -> RPIE, the runtime random pie built by GNBASE (:1059-1075, which copies the PIEXX skeleton then fills tail slots via `LDB P.RND1 / MUL` from TWDGXX). Exactly the claim: waves 0-10 fixed, 11+ random. Our spawnTrenchObstacles (trench-obstacles.ts:173-179) re-picks TRENCH_TAIL_POOL for every station i >= TRENCH_HEAD_COUNT on EVERY run with no wave gating, so it never reproduces the deterministic authored layout the ROM flies for BS.WAV 0..10. The finding also correctly credits our fixed-head/picked-tail model as the RIGHT mechanism for the RANDOM pie (GNBASE), merely misapplied to all waves. Holds.

Decoded the WFF collision in WSPANL and it held. The 'catwalk' is TD$WFF, panel type 2 in the B-010 grid — a WALL force field, not a channel-spanning bar. It is side-gated: PNVLW (left wall) fires only `LDD M$TY+M.U1 / IFLE ;?ON LEFT SIDE?` (:198-199); PNVRW (right wall) only `LDD M$TY+M.U1 / IFGE ;?ON RIGHT SIDE?` (:366-367). It occupies one discrete stacked vertical slot on the Y=+/-400 wall, gated in a vertical band (`M.Z0 ADDD #200` top, `SUBD #400` bottom) and only within the field's first $400 of depth (`SUBD #400 ;HALF OF FF DEPTH / IFLS`). On a hit the ROM does `LDA #TD$WFG` (brighten), `JSR BG1GLW` (glow ship), `JSR AUDCR` (crash sound), and sets S.ROL (roll) — a graze, with no lives/shield decrement in this routine (shield accounting is in WSGLOW). Ours (sim.ts:648-664, 682-688): a single catwalk authored at pos [0, CATWALK_Y=888, z] — centered, full channel width — collides with the ship within CATWALK_HIT_RADIUS regardless of side, and immediately does `lives = base.lives - 1` (can trigger gameOver). Because the ROM hazard is side-gated by M$TY sign and slotted to a height, a pilot clamped to +/-511 inside +/-1024 walls can steer clear laterally; our x=0 full-width bar can only be dodged by diving, and it costs a shield outright. Confirmed on every sub-claim.
corrections: Overlap note (not a defect): B-012 and B-010 describe the SAME wedge/panel grid from two angles — B-010 the encoding, B-012 one panel type's collision behaviour. The finding itself states 'reconciling requires porting the wall-slot panel grid (B-010)', so a single rewrite fixes both. Distinct claims, both TRUE; not a duplicate filing.

reasoning: Traced the B0B phase path and it held. WSMAIN:1896-1914: once BS.EFL is set and the end wall closes to `BS.ELC - M$TX - #0800 <= 0` ('ABOUT TO BASH OUR NOSE IN THE END WALL'), it reads `LDA PT.LIV`; on a MISS (PT.LIV=0) it sets Q.BSH, plays AUDCR + BG1GLW, and — if shields remain (S.GAS>0) — does `LDA #PH$B0B / STA PHASE` + SPKR2N ('R2 SWEARS AT PLAYER FOR MISSING'). PH$B0B routes to PHIB0B (:1806-1827): `LDA #1 / STA BS.RPT ;REPEATING THIS WAVE`, `JSR IPARM`, then `JSR NWBASE ;JUST INIT THE CURRENT SELECTION` with the explicit comment 'DO NOT GENERATE A NEW BASE' — NWBASE re-reads the unchanged BS.WAV and re-inits the SAME pie from the start; `LDA #0FF / STA Q.FRC ;NO FORCE ALLOWED ON RERUNS`; bumps WV.HRD (harder on repeat); then PH$BS to re-fly the whole trench. So the ROM re-flies the entire same trench from the trench start. Ours (sim.ts:780-794): when the port reaches the fixed COCKPIT within COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS, `lives-1` + `exhaustPort: spawnPort()` — a single fresh port respawns 'another pass down the trench' while the channel keeps scrolling; the trench body/obstacles never reset. The finding's secondary point also holds: our miss uses the fixed COCKPIT point + COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS, not the ROM's PT.LIV-read-at-the-$800-end-wall-gate decision. Confirmed, including the no-Force-on-reruns detail.

Decoded the floor-draw table and routine and it held. TBSBL 'BASE BOTTOM LINES' (WSBASE:1480-1489) is six (Y,Z) rows: two top rails at Y=-400/+400 (Z=0) and four bottom lines at Y=-400 (FAR LEFT BOTTOM), -200 (LEFT THIRD), +200 (RIGHT THIRD), +400 (FAR RIGHT BOTTOM), all Z=-1000. BSVBOT (:1409-1478) draws each row as ONE LONGITUDINAL line: it fixes M.Y0/M.Z0 from the table and strokes from near (M.X0=#200) to far (M.X0=#7000) at that constant (Y,Z) via BJLNV. So the ROM floor is exactly FOUR longitudinal lines at Y=+/-400 and +/-200; the walls are drawn separately as vertical lines at wedge boundaries (BSVSDW), and NOTHING draws a lateral floor rib spanning the width. Ours (trench-channel.ts:126-157): four longitudinal rails at x=+/-TRENCH_HALF_W (two floor y=0 + two top y=TRENCH_WALL_H), then at each RIB_Z it pushes a LATERAL floor rib across the channel (`fl`-`fr`, :146-148) — an element the ROM never draws — plus vertical wall ribs; and it omits the ROM's inner +/-$200 (=+/-512) floor 'thirds' lines. Every clause of the finding checks out. Cosmetic/visual per the finding's own 'accept' recommendation, but the divergence is real.
